title: respondent-annotator
description: Annotation extension for respondent procedures. Documents the four respondent-related annotations — @cs, @us, @iti, @cs_interval — that attach metadata to respondent primitives and to phases that host composed operant × respondent procedures. The extension lives under annotations/extensions/ because respondent stimulus and timing metadata cross-cut the four JEAB-aligned categories (Subjects / Apparatus / Procedure / Measurement).

cs.description: CS (conditional stimulus) metadata. Attachable to any Phase or respondent expression; inherited from PhaseSequence.shared_annotations through the standard keyword-override rule. Adds metadata to the cs parameter of Pair.*, Extinction, CSOnly, Compound, Serial, and Differential.

label.description: CS identifier used in the respondent primitive (e.g. 'tone', 'light'). Must match the cs reference used in the corresponding Pair.*, Extinction, CSOnly, Compound, Serial, or Differential primitive.

duration.description: Nominal CS duration. Reproduces (redundantly, for readability) the cs_duration argument of Pair.ForwardDelay when present; required for respondent primitives that do not themselves carry a cs_duration argument.

modality.description: Sensory modality of the CS. Enumerated recommendations: 'auditory', 'visual', 'olfactory', 'tactile'. The enumeration is not enforced by the grammar; programs may extend it.

errors.CS_LABEL_REQUIRED: label is required for apparatus-verification and for matching against the primitive's cs reference
errors.CS_DURATION_NONPOSITIVE: duration, if specified, must be strictly positive
errors.CS_LABEL_UNMATCHED: label does not match any cs reference in the enclosing respondent primitive

cs.reference: Pavlov, I. P. (1927). Conditioned reflexes: An investigation of the physiological activity of the cerebral cortex (G. V. Anrep, Trans.). Oxford University Press.

us.description: US (unconditional stimulus) metadata. Attachable to any Phase or respondent expression; inherited through the standard override rule. Adds metadata to the us parameter of any respondent primitive that carries a US reference (Pair.*, USOnly, Differential). Also useful on composed-layer procedures (CER, PIT) where the US is specified in the operant context but drives Pavlovian learning.

label.description: US identifier matching the us reference in the corresponding primitive (e.g. 'shock', 'food').

intensity.description: US intensity specification. Free-form string to accommodate heterogeneous units (e.g. '0.5mA', '45mg_pellet', '3s_access'). Production programs parse this string per apparatus configuration.

delivery.description: US delivery mode. Enumerated recommendations: 'unsignaled', 'signaled', 'response_contingent', 'cancelled_on_cs_response'. The enumeration is not enforced by the grammar; programs may extend it.

errors.US_LABEL_REQUIRED: label is required for matching against the primitive's us reference
errors.US_LABEL_UNMATCHED: label does not match any us reference in the enclosing respondent primitive

us.reference: Rescorla, R. A. (1967). Pavlovian conditioning and its proper control procedures. Psychological Review, 74(1), 71-80. https://doi.org/10.1037/h0024109

iti.description: Intertrial-interval metadata with jitter. Attachable to Phase or PhaseSequence (shared inheritance); also to trial-based respondent blocks. Distinct from the ITI(distribution, mean) primitive (respondent R13): the primitive is a structural declaration, the annotation carries jitter information (additional detail not required by the primitive's operational definition).

distribution.description: ITI distribution family. Matches the ITI primitive's distribution enum.

jitter.description: Range or standard deviation of ITI variability around the mean. jitter=0 denotes no jitter.

errors.ITI_JITTER_NEGATIVE: jitter, if specified, must be non-negative
errors.ITI_MEAN_NONPOSITIVE: mean, if specified, must be strictly positive

iti.reference: Gallistel, C. R., & Gibbon, J. (2000). Time, rate, and conditioning. Psychological Review, 107(2), 289-344. https://doi.org/10.1037/0033-295X.107.2.289

cs_interval.description: CS presentation time window. Attachable to Pair primitives (Pair.ForwardDelay, Pair.ForwardTrace, Pair.Simultaneous, Pair.Backward) and to phases hosting these primitives. Makes the CS presentation window explicit for apparatus-verification and for compilation into natural-language Method / Procedure sections. The temporal window is already encoded in the primitive's parameters; cs_interval records it in a reader-friendly form.

cs_onset.description: Time of CS onset relative to trial start (typically 0-s).

cs_offset.description: Time of CS offset relative to trial start.

errors.CS_INTERVAL_OFFSET_NOT_AFTER_ONSET: cs_offset must be strictly greater than cs_onset
errors.CS_INTERVAL_INCONSISTENT_WITH_PRIMITIVE: cs_offset - cs_onset does not match the enclosing primitive's cs_duration

cs_interval.reference: Ellison, G. D. (1964). Differential salivary conditioning to traces. Journal of Comparative and Physiological Psychology, 57(3), 373-380. https://doi.org/10.1037/h0043484
